summary: Stellar 1984 soundboard capture with a smoking Bird Song/Dancin' opener and a powerful Dark Star, though a few mix quirks surface.
review: The recording is an A+‑grade 1984 SBD that delivers crisp, clear sound for almost the entire set, only occasionally burying Garcia’s guitar. Musically the show shines – the Dancin’ > Bird Song opening, a soaring Bird Song, a fiery Dark Star and tight China Cat → Rider jams make it a highlight of the Greek run, with most fans calling it one of the best ’80s performances despite minor complaints about Cumberland and a single weak song.

ai_rating.rationale: 23 reviews overwhelmingly positive (average 4.39 stars). The majority praise both performance and recording quality; only a handful of dissenting comments affect the overall sentiment, yielding a strong but not perfect rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp, clear SBD with A+ fidelity. Reviewers note occasional mix issues – Garcia’s guitar sometimes sits low in the front, but overall the sound is balanced and very listenable.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show kicks off with a joyous Dancin’ into Bird Song, delivering a high‑energy first set that builds to a fiery Dark Star in the second. Seamless transitions through China Cat → Rider and the Playin' / UJB combo keep momentum, while the band stays tight throughout.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is described as “buzzing”, “scorching” and “mind‑bending,” though a few reviewers note his guitar sits low in the mix on parts of the set.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s bass is praised for “dropping bombs” and providing a strong rhythmic foundation; fans love his active role in the China Cat jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby is mentioned positively (“bobby being bobby!”) with no specific criticism beyond a brief road‑crew reference at the start of Brokedown Palace.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards receive glowing praise – “shines,” “sparkling,” and “greatest keyboard player of the period” – but one reviewer explicitly dislikes his voice/keyboard style, giving a single negative viewpoint.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drummers (Mickey/Bill) are lauded for “insane energy pre‑drums,” “crash cymbal happy,” and solid reading throughout the set.
